arguments.task: Open the Mermaid CLI GitHub repository and gather evidence from first-party sources about: what Mermaid CLI is, whether it has any authentication requirements, whether access is self-serve or gated, whether it exposes a REST or GraphQL API or no API surface, installation/access details from npm if linked, any rate limit notes, and whether an MCP server exists from official or highly authoritative sources such as GitHub or npm. Also inspect linked official docs or package pages if available, and return the supporting URLs with concise findings.

raw_result.description: Command-line tool for rendering Mermaid diagram definitions to SVG, PNG, or PDF outputs.
raw_result.auth_notes: Public open-source CLI/package for local use; no user authentication flow or API credential scheme was evidenced in the gathered first-party sources, so auth is recorded as other/non-applicable.
raw_result.gating_reason: Repository and npm package are publicly available under MIT license and installable directly via npm or Docker without approval flow.

api_surface.breadth: No public REST or GraphQL service surface was evidenced; gathered evidence indicates a local CLI and package usage rather than a hosted API.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Browser evidence found no official MCP repository in the mermaid-js GitHub organization, but absence is not fully proven across all ecosystems.

raw_result.blocker: No documented hosted API surface was evidenced; toolkit would likely need to wrap the local CLI/package execution rather than integrate a standard remote API.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit information was evidenced in the gathered sources, consistent with a local CLI/package rather than a hosted API.
raw_result.notable_signals: GitHub repository indicates Mermaid CLI converts Mermaid definitions and is MIT-licensed; npm package is public and self-serve; browser run noted npm and Docker availability; no public REST/GraphQL API or official MCP server was evidenced from gathered first-party/authoritative pages.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Auth is effectively non-applicable for local CLI use, but schema requires a listed auth method; MCP absence and exact package/API breadth were concluded from negative evidence gathered so far and should be human-checked if strict certainty is required.
